excerpt: When the first intellect and the first source met, they invited others to Tawheed, just as both hands clap and the sound affects others.
source_location_hint: On the cosmic invitation and the emanation of intellects

excerpt: The main purpose of the invitation was to remove the mischief of the second source, as when part of a cloth is dirty, the whole cloth is washed but cleaning concentrates at the point of dirt.
source_location_hint: On the cosmic invitation and the role of the second emanator

excerpt: Seven intellects were established in correspondence to the seven letters of Surat al-Ikhlas, each acknowledging the tawhid of the Creator and the precedence of the intellect before it.
source_location_hint: On the structure of the seven intellects and their cosmic correspondence

excerpt: When the circle's number reached twenty-seven, or according to some texts one hundred, the last member of the circle was commanded to issue the daʿwa.
source_location_hint: On the formation of circles and the transmission of the daʿwa

excerpt: The actions in the spiritual world correspond to alif, lam, and ha: the intellect advances from itself (alif), another advances by witnessing (lam), and at the daʿwa of the first source, seven intellects advance (ha).
source_location_hint: On the esoteric structure of the Divine Name and the seven intellects

excerpt: When the seven intellects were established, the First Intellect manifested as radiant luminescence. Witnessing this, all intellects acknowledged the First Intellect and conferred upon Him the Name Allah, as mentioned in Quran 3:18.
source_location_hint: On the testimony of God, angels, and the knowers, and the cosmic recognition of the First Intellect
